LOOK.text: An ancient CRT monitor with a green phosphor glow. The screen shows a blinking cursor: 'INSERT ADRIAN'S LEDGER TO CONTINUE...'

then.text: The computer shows the patient roster. 'PATIENT ZERO — STATUS: ESCAPED' blinks in red at the bottom.
I insert the floppy disk... *KRRRTTT*... The drive spins up. Data flooding the screen.
The roster lists patients. Conditions read like a crypto obituary:
Chronic Airdrop Dependency... Acute Rug Pull Trauma... Terminal FOMO...
At the bottom, in red ink: PATIENT ZERO — STATUS: ESCAPED
Escaped? Through what? And to where?
The printer spits out a map. This could lead somewhere.

else.text: The computer is already unlocked. The screen shows Adrian's private vault dashboard. The disk drive blinks, waiting for something to read.
I plug in Adrian's Ledger... The screen flashes: 'WALLET RECOGNIZED. VAULT ACCESS GRANTED.'
The monitor reveals a dashboard: portfolio tracker, NFT collection, and... a map to something called 'Patient Zero Clinic'.
The screen says 'INSERT ADRIAN'S LEDGER'. I need to find it somewhere.

TALK.text: The CRT buzzes: 'Been mining since block zero... still waiting for my moment.'

PICK.text: The computer is bolted to the desk. It's not going anywhere, just like my unrealized gains.

OPEN.text: I open the disk drive. Empty. This machine is waiting for something to read.

CLOSE.text: I close the disk drive. The CRT continues its eternal green glow.

PUSH.text: I push a key on the keyboard. The CRT flickers. 'DO NOT MASH KEYS' appears on screen.

PULL.text: I pull the keyboard closer. A cockroach scurries out. Even bugs live on-chain now.

GIVE.text: The CRT doesn't accept gifts. It only accepts Adrian's Ledger and floppy disks.

LOOK.text: Wooden stairs leading back up. They creak ominously with each step.

TALK.text: The stairs creak. I interpret that as 'careful, these are old'.

PICK.text: A loose nail sticks out of one step. Tetanus is always an adventure, but I'll pass.

CLOSE.text: Stairs don't close. They're always open for business.

PUSH.text: I push the banister. It wobbles. This whole place is held together by hope and duct tape.

PULL.text: I pull a loose board. Splinters. That's what I get for interacting with legacy infrastructure.

GIVE.text: Stairs don't accept offerings. They're not a gas fee.

LOOK.text: A small desk with a tiny monitor and some chairs. Papers scattered everywhere. Someone was working late.

USE.text: I sit down and look at the papers. Mostly scribbled calculations and the word 'WAGMI' circled repeatedly.

TALK.text: I talk to the desk lamp. It flickers sympathetically.

then.text: I already grabbed everything useful from this desk.

else.text: I find a floppy disk hidden under the papers! The label reads: 'BACKUP - DO NOT LOSE'

then.text: I already grabbed everything useful from this desk.

else.text: I open the desk drawer. Under old receipts and dust... a 3.5 inch floppy labeled 'BACKUP'.

CLOSE.text: I close the drawer. The dust settles back.

PUSH.text: I push the desk. Papers fly everywhere. WAGMI notes scatter like failed transactions.

PULL.text: I pull the desk drawer out completely. Just a dead spider and a mint condition 'Buy Bitcoin' sticker.

GIVE.text: The desk doesn't want anything. It already has too much on its plate. Literally.

LOOK.text: A single light bulb hanging from a wire. It swings slightly, casting dancing shadows. Very noir.

USE.text: I pull the chain. The light flickers but stays on. Persistent, like a good miner.

TALK.text: I stare into the light. It stares back. We have an understanding.

PICK.text: I'm not touching a bare light bulb. I've made bad decisions but not THAT bad.

OPEN.text: I can't open a light bulb. Well, I could, but it would cease to be a light bulb.

CLOSE.text: I pull the chain again. Darkness. Then I pull it again. Light! Fun.

PUSH.text: I push the bulb. It swings like a pendulum. The shadows dance. Spooky proof-of-swing.

PULL.text: I pull the wire. The light drops an inch. Now it's a slightly lower hanging light.

GIVE.text: I can't give energy to a light bulb. That's the electrician's job.

LOOK.text: Speakers, an old amp, and various electronic equipment. The early days of the lab's setup.

USE.text: I flip a switch on the amp. A low hum fills the basement. Vintage vibes.

TALK.text: I talk into a microphone connected to the amp. My voice echoes: 'Hello? Is this thing on?'

PICK.text: The equipment is all wired together. I'd need hours to untangle it.

OPEN.text: I open the equipment cabinet. Cables, adapters, and a lone AUX cable. The holy grail.

CLOSE.text: I close the cabinet. The cables spill out again. Entropy wins.

else.text: The rack slides aside with a groan. Behind it — a hidden door. Still warm from the servers.
Someone didn't want this room found.

PULL.text: I pull an AUX cable. The entire rack shifts. Everything's connected, like a blockchain.

GIVE.text: The amp doesn't accept donations. It only accepts sick guitar riffs.

LOOK.text: A worn leather armchair. It's seen better days. And worse trades.

USE.text: I sit down. The cushion sighs under my weight. We share a moment of mutual exhaustion.

TALK.text: I whisper to the armchair: 'What secrets have you heard?' It creaks in response.

else.text: Wait... something just appeared in my pocket. A sticker? It shows a three-headed monkey. 'LOOK BEHIND YOU!' it says. Where did this come from?!

PICK.text: I check between the cushions... a coin! Just a regular quarter though. 0.25 USD. Off-chain.

OPEN.text: I flip the cushion. Nothing but crumbs and regret.

CLOSE.text: I put the cushion back. The chair returns to its default state.

PUSH.text: I push the armchair. It slides, revealing years of dust bunnies. The original NFTs — Non-Fungible Tumbleweeds.

PULL.text: I pull the armrest. It comes off. I put it back. Pretend that didn't happen.

GIVE.text: The armchair has received enough. Mostly butts and bad news.

LOOK.text: Old checkered tile floor. Some tiles are cracked. Each crack tells a story of a late-night coding session.

USE.text: I stomp on a loose tile. It wobbles. Just a wobbly tile, nothing hidden.

TALK.text: My footsteps echo on the tiles. The basement acknowledges my presence.

PICK.text: I pry at a loose tile. Just dirt underneath. No secret passages.

OPEN.text: I check for trapdoors. Nothing. This is the bottom level.

CLOSE.text: The floor is as closed as it gets.

PUSH.text: I push a tile with my foot. It clicks. Nothing happens. Anticlimactic.

PULL.text: I pull up a loose tile. Dirt, worms, and a bottle cap. The original Layer 0.

GIVE.text: I can't give the floor anything it doesn't already have. Mostly dust and footprints.

then.text: The mouse cable still dangles from the desk. A reminder of its sacrifice.

else.text: A computer mouse dangling off the edge of the desk by its severed cable. It has seen better days.

USE.text: I try to click it. The left button barely responds. This mouse is beyond repair.

TALK.text: 'Click?' Nothing. 'Double-click?' Silence. This mouse has clicked its last.

then.text: Just a dangling cable left. The mouse has moved on to a better inventory.

else.text: I grab the mouse. The cable snaps. Well, it was already broken. Now it's portable-broken.

OPEN.text: I pop open the mouse shell. Inside: a dusty scroll wheel and shattered dreams.

CLOSE.text: I snap the shell back. It doesn't click shut anymore. Ironic for a mouse.

PUSH.text: I push the mouse across the desk. It slides without resistance. No friction pad left.

PULL.text: I pull the dangling cable. The mouse swings like a sad pendulum.

GIVE.text: I wouldn't give this mouse to my worst enemy. Actually, maybe to that one guy in the Telegram chat.

LOOK.text: Scraps of paper pinned to the wall. Scrawled diagrams, wallet addresses, and one note that just says 'TRUST NO ONE' in red marker.

USE.text: I try to decode the diagrams. It's either a smart contract architecture or a conspiracy board. Hard to tell the difference.

TALK.text: The notes rustle in the draft. They're whispering alpha. Or just whispering.

PICK.text: I pull one off the wall. It reads: 'If you're reading this, check block 4,206,969.' I put it back.

OPEN.text: I unfold one note. Inside: a seed phrase. Just kidding. It's a grocery list. Ramen, energy drinks, more ramen.

CLOSE.text: I fold the note back up. Some secrets are better left crumpled.

PUSH.text: I push the thumbtack deeper. The note holds firm. Unlike most stablecoins.

PULL.text: I pull a note off. Underneath is another note that says 'STOP PULLING NOTES OFF THE WALL.'

GIVE.text: These notes belong to the wall. It's a decentralized bulletin board.

LOOK.text: Rusty pipes and wooden beams run across the ceiling. They drip occasionally. The basement's own liquidity pool.

USE.text: I tap a pipe. It clangs and echoes through the basement. Somewhere above, someone probably thinks there's a ghost.

TALK.text: The pipes gurgle. In plumber Morse code, that means 'maintenance overdue since 2019.'

PICK.text: I'm not ripping pipes out of the ceiling. I've seen what happens when you break infrastructure. It's called 'a bridge exploit.'

OPEN.text: I twist a valve. Water spurts out, then stops. The pipe wheezes. That was close.

CLOSE.text: I close the valve tighter. The dripping stops. First thing I've successfully closed since my leveraged position.

PUSH.text: I push on a pipe. It bows slightly. These pipes have load-bearing anxiety, just like me.

PULL.text: I pull a loose pipe fitting. Rust flakes rain down. Proof-of-decay.

GIVE.text: The pipes want a plumber, not an adventurer. But they'll take what they can get.

LOOK.text: Rough stone walls. Old, solid, unyielding. Like a whale's conviction during a market crash.

USE.text: I run my hand along the stone. Cold and damp. This basement predates the internet, let alone the blockchain.

TALK.text: I talk to the wall. It's more responsive than most Discord mods.

PICK.text: I pry at a loose stone. It doesn't budge. These walls were built to last, like a proper Layer 1.

OPEN.text: No hidden passages here. Real life isn't Monkey Island. ...or is it?

CLOSE.text: The walls are already closed. That's kind of their whole thing.

PUSH.text: I push the wall. The wall wins. Immutability: 1, Me: 0.

PULL.text: I pull at a stone. My fingernails disagree with this plan. Strongly.

GIVE.text: The walls don't accept bribes. Truly decentralized governance.

lines: Beneath the lab, forgotten machines still process blocks.
A CRT monitor flickers with data from another era.
The patient roster... the escape... Patient Zero.
The answers are down here. Buried in code.

then.text: The computer already recognized Adrian's Ledger. The vault dashboard is still open.

else.text: I plug in Adrian's Ledger... The screen flashes: 'WALLET RECOGNIZED. VAULT ACCESS GRANTED.'
The monitor reveals a dashboard: portfolio tracker, NFT collection, and... a map to something called 'Patient Zero Clinic'.

then.text: The CRT still displays Adrian's hidden message. The cursor blinks patiently, like it has all the time in the world. Because it does. It's a computer.

else.text: I slide a floppy from the box into the drive. The CRT goes dark. Then...
*KRRRRTTTT-KRRRRTTTT* The drive spins up like it's been WAITING for this exact disc. The screen floods with green text.
'ADRIANLAB FLOPPY COLLECTION DETECTED — HOLDER OVERRIDE ACTIVE. Decrypting Sector Zero...'
A hidden partition opens. Adrian left a personal message encoded in the floppy metadata:
'To whoever holds these discs: you're not just a collector. You backed the lab when nobody believed pixel art on a blockchain was worth anything. You were right. — A.'
Below the message, a manifest: every floppy disc ever minted, every pack opened, every trait unlocked. The entire AdrianLAB creation history. On a 1.44MB floppy. Somehow.
The CRT prints a final line: 'COMPRESSION RATIO: infinity. When you believe in the art, size doesn't matter.'
SECTOR ZERO — Unlocked Adrian's hidden floppy message

lines: Beneath the lab, forgotten machines still process blocks.
A CRT monitor flickers with data from another era.
The patient roster... the escape... Patient Zero.
The answers are down here. Buried in code.
